evals.name: hilt-network-module
evals.expected_output: Should follow the 8-step migration workflow. Step 1: Inventory the NetworkModule bindings (3 singletons + qualifier). Step 2: Plan mapping @Provides/@Singleton → single{}, @AuthInterceptor → named("auth"). Step 3: Gradle changes removing Hilt deps, adding Koin deps. Step 4: Generate Koin module with single{} blocks, named("auth") qualifier. Step 5: startKoin{} setup. Step 6: ViewModel conversion from @HiltViewModel to viewModelOf() with SavedStateHandle. Step 7: Test config. Step 8: Verification checklist.

evals.name: dagger-subcomponents-scopes
evals.prompt: We have a legacy Android app using Dagger 2 with subcomponents for user sessions. The AppComponent creates a UserSessionSubcomponent when the user logs in, which provides UserRepository and SessionManager scoped to the session lifetime. When the user logs out, the subcomponent is destroyed. How do I migrate this to Koin while keeping the same scoping behavior?
evals.expected_output: Should follow 8-step workflow. Step 1: Inventory AppComponent + UserSessionSubcomponent bindings. Step 2: Plan mapping subcomponent → scope<T>, @SessionScope → scope<UserSessionScope>{}. Step 3: Gradle remove Dagger, add Koin. Step 4: Generate scope<UserSessionScope>{ scoped{SessionManager(get())} scoped{UserRepository(get())} }. Step 5: startKoin setup. Step 6: Replace component.inject() with by inject(), createScope on login, scope.close() on logout. Step 7: Test with KoinTestRule. Step 8: Checklist.

evals.name: koin3-to-koin4-kmp
evals.prompt: I'm upgrading our KMP project from Koin 3.5 to Koin 4. We have common modules and platform-specific modules for Android and iOS. We also use koin-annotations with KSP. What do I need to change?
evals.expected_output: Should follow 8-step workflow. Step 1: Inventory existing Koin modules, KSP setup, platform modules. Step 2: Plan — upgrade to Safe DSL single<T>() syntax, remove KSP. Step 3: Gradle — add io.insert-koin.compiler.plugin, update to Koin 4.2.1, remove KSP plugin and koin-ksp-compiler, require Kotlin ≥ 2.3.20. Step 4: Convert singleOf(::Class) to single<Class>(), factoryOf(::Class) to factory<Class>(), viewModelOf(::VM) to viewModel<VM>(), keep lambda for third-party types. Step 5: Application wiring with KoinContext for Compose. Step 6: getViewModel → koinViewModel, get() → koinInject() in Compose. Step 7: Test with checkModules(). Step 8: Checklist. Module structure stays as val module = module { }.

evals.name: toothpick-full-migration
evals.prompt: We have a legacy Android app still using Toothpick for DI. It uses KTP.openScopes with a scope tree: Application → Activity → Fragment. Each scope has its own Toothpick Module with bind().to() and bind().toInstance(). We also have @Inject field injection everywhere and a custom ViewModelProvider.Factory wired through Toothpick. How do I migrate all of this to Koin?
evals.expected_output: Should follow 8-step workflow. Step 1: Inventory all Toothpick Modules, scope tree, @Inject fields, ViewModelFactory. Step 2: Plan mapping bind().to() → singleOf(::Impl){bind<Interface>()}, KTP.openScopes → scope<T>{}, @Inject → by inject(). Step 3: Gradle remove toothpick/smoothie, add Koin. Step 4: Convert each Toothpick Module to Koin module{}, scope<Activity>{scoped{}}, scope<Fragment>{scoped{}}. Step 5: startKoin in Application, KoinScopeComponent for scoped Activities/Fragments. Step 6: Replace @Inject lateinit var with by inject(), remove Toothpick.inject() calls, remove ViewModelFactory. Step 7: Test migration from ToothPickRule to KoinTestRule. Step 8: Full cleanup checklist.

evals.name: koin-dsl-to-safe-dsl
evals.expected_output: Should follow 8-step workflow. Step 1: Inventory the 2 shown modules (4 bindings in dataModule, 2 in viewModelModule). Step 2: Plan — keep module {} structure, convert to Safe DSL single<T>() syntax. Step 3: Gradle add io.insert-koin.compiler.plugin, update to Koin 4.2.1, require Kotlin ≥ 2.3.20. Step 4: Convert dataModule — keep lambda for AppDatabase.create() and userDao() (third-party/custom logic), convert singleOf(::UserRepository) to single<UserRepository>().withOptions { bind<Repository>() }, convert factory to factory<DataSync>() with @InjectedParam on DataSync's runtime param. Convert viewModelModule — viewModelOf(::MainViewModel) to viewModel<MainViewModel>(), viewModel{DetailViewModel(get(),get())} to viewModel<DetailViewModel>(). Step 5: startKoin unchanged. Step 6: Only add @InjectedParam to DataSync class. Step 7: checkModules() test. Step 8: Checklist.

evals.name: ksp-to-compiler-plugin
evals.expected_output: Should follow 8-step workflow. Step 1: Inventory existing KSP annotations and modules. Step 2: Plan — mostly Gradle changes, minimal code changes (@KoinViewModel import). Step 3: Gradle — remove KSP plugin, remove ksp() dependency, remove ksp{} block, add io.insert-koin.compiler.plugin 1.0.0-RC1, update all deps to 4.2.1. Step 4: Code changes — update @KoinViewModel import from org.koin.android.annotation to org.koin.core.annotation, remove import org.koin.ksp.generated.module, optionally update DSL to single<T>() style. Step 5: Application wiring stays same. Step 6: Minimal injection site changes. Step 7: Tests with updated deps. Step 8: Checklist including delete build/generated/ksp/, verify K2 compilation.

evals.name: ksp-to-compiler-kmp
evals.expected_output: Should follow 8-step workflow. Step 1: Inventory KMP modules, per-target KSP config. Step 2: Plan massive simplification. Step 3: Gradle — remove KSP plugin, ALL per-target ksp() dependencies, source set hack, task dependency hack; add single io.insert-koin.compiler.plugin; update to koin 4.2.1. Step 4: Minimal code changes (import updates). Step 5: Application wiring. Step 6: Injection sites. Step 7: Tests. Step 8: Checklist. Should emphasize the dramatic build.gradle.kts simplification as a key benefit.
